text: asked around a mouthful of jerky . Ronin nodded. He was leaning against the wall beside the table, arms folded over his chest . “Why’d you come back early ?” “Because I didn’t like the thought of you here, alone .” It was the last answer she expected. He’d been worried about her. Before they coupled , as he put it, before their physical closeness betrayed the possibility of deeper feelings for one another, he had been thinking about her safety. She’d have preferred he not leave at all , but … During her time here, she’d noticed Ronin watching her often. If they were in the same room, his eyes were locked on her, rarely looking away. She’d pretend not to notice. It would have been easy to dismiss it as some creepy bot behavior, but she knew better now. There was more beneath the surface than she would’ve believed . Had Tabitha shared something similar with the bot who took her in? Mutual curiosity, mutual admiration, care for one another’s wellbeing? Maybe it hadn’t been only for the shelter, food, and security. What if…what if Tabitha had been happy ? The thought gave Lara pause. Was she happy? It seemed wrong, in the face of her sister’s death, but beneath the gloom of her grief, there was light. Why else would it be there, if not for Ronin? She couldn’t guess what drew him to her — there were many prettier women, in her opinion, even without counting the female synths — but his interest was genuine. He wanted her safe, wanted her content . Did Tabitha’s bot try to protect her , too ? Obviously, it had failed, in the end…but how could it have succeeded? Even without the details, she knew in her gut that Warlord was responsible. No single person, human or bot, could stand against Cheyenne’s ruler and his thugs . Why? Why had they murdered Tabitha and her companion ? “Am I in danger, Ronin ?” “Everyone is always in some degree of danger .” “But you came back. Something must’ve crossed your mind, for you to come back after arguing with me that you needed more time out there .” “I had a sizeable haul, and I realized that I’d rather be with you than digging through the dirt for a few more pieces of scrap .” Heat bloomed on her cheeks, and she dropped her gaze to the worktable. There was more to it, she guessed, that he wasn’t saying. But that didn’t change how she felt. She took in a deep breath and released it slowly. “I missed you. I know you weren’t gone for long, but I got used to you being around, and…I missed you .” He was silent. After a few moments, she looked up at him Head tilted to the side, he stared for a while longer. “Missed,” he finally said. “That’s a good term. It was like I was missing a part of myself.” He pushed off the wall to stand up straight, and reached into his pants pocket. “There’s one more thing I found .” Ronin held out his fist and uncurled his fingers. A ring lay on his palm, the band gleaming in the morning light. It was familiar, from a lifetime ago, but it had only been days since she last saw it . “I think this is yours, Lara Brooks .” Lara stared at the small metal band in disbelief. “How did you ….” “When I left town, I passed the place where you were scavenging in the rain. I stopped, caught up in the memory, when I noticed this buried in the debris .” “I thought it was gone.” She carefully took the ring between her forefinger and thumb and lifted it from his palm, half-expecting it to disappear. Though slight, it had weight; it was real. She grinned. “Thank you. You know, when a guy gives a girl a ring, it’s usually because he’s asking her to marry him. Never seen one as fine as this .” His brow furrowed, and he tilted his head again. “I fail to understand why humans still follow that practice. Isn’t it just the recognition of a coupling by a representative of authority? And what does a ring have to do with it, anyway ?” “Quite the romantic, aren’t you?” She curled her fingers around the ring. “It’s tradition. Something people have held onto despite…you know, all this . There’s hardship, yeah, but people still fall in love .” Gary and Kate came to mind immediately. They were always affectionate and supportive with one another . “I don’t know what you mean about...well, that other stuff you said. The hell does it mean, anyway?” she asked . The corners of his mouth dipped. “My data is incomplete. Most of the relevant information is lost in the corrupted portion of my memory. There used to be…” He lapsed into silence for several seconds before shaking his head. “Can’t remember. Just means the people in charge had to acknowledge a marriage, before. You mentioned love. Is marriage required for love ?” Could he ever regain his lost memories, or were they gone forever ? “Um, no. It’s not. Some people just like tradition, I guess. I think it’s because we’ve already lost so much. Feels good to keep something old alive.” She held the ring on her open hand, and ran a finger around its loop . “Someone told me once that a ring means eternity, because it’s a circle, and circles never end. A man finds something to use as a ring — wire, or some cheap bit of metal, or even just some twine — and gives it to a woman when he asks her to be his wife. If she says yes, they both wear a ring, to show they are taken and devoted to each other. They make their vows to each other, usually with some other people watching, and then
